domain.title: Avian influenza and bird mortality (India)
domain.description: Indian news articles reporting avian influenza (HPAI/LPAI), mass bird mortality, domestic poultry outbreaks, and human bird-flu cases. Wild birds, farm poultry, backyard flocks, live-bird markets, environmental mass mortality, and mixed One Health contexts are in scope. Taxonomy fields bird_type and habitat_type distinguish wild vs poultry hosts and farm vs wetland vs environmental settings. Pipeline: gdelt-fetch-urls.py → gdelt-enrich-urls-bigquery.py → gdelt-get-full-text.py → convert_csv_to_geojson.py. LLM JSON uses is_hwc_event (CSV column name, shared with HWC pipeline) as the boolean 'include this article' flag.

gdelt_doc_fetch._comment_keywords: Keywords anchored on the mortality event itself, not the disease name. Indian-specific terms (Pong Dam, bar-headed geese, wetland sites) supplement generic terms. 'bird flu' and 'avian influenza' are included because Indian news frequently reports the disease name alongside the mortality event.
keywords: poultry birds dead avian influenza India
gdelt_doc_fetch._comment_fetch_dates: Inclusive DOC API window; same convention as meta/hwc_india_conflict_meta.json. Omit fetch_end_date or use empty string for 'through today' (UTC date).
gdelt_doc_fetch.summary_banner: GDELT Avian Influenza / Bird Mortality — India
gdelt_doc_fetch.summary_title: GDELT Avian Influenza India — Feasibility Summary

bigquery_gkg_fetch._comment: gdelt-fetch-urls.py --source bigquery: URL slug LIKE proxies on DocumentIdentifier + India V2Locations + exclusions. Theme scoring for those URLs still uses gkg_theme_sets (primary wildlife + secondary disease/mortality) in gdelt-enrich-urls-bigquery.py. partition_time_* is the GKG scan window; 2021 start may lag gdelt_doc_fetch 2020 to limit query cost—align both when you need identical calendar coverage.

gkg_theme_sets._comment_design: PRIMARY set = wildlife/bird domain signal. SECONDARY set = disease/pathogen/mortality signal. The two sets are strictly mutually exclusive — no theme appears in both. Score 3 (both sets match) = high-confidence article about bird disease or mortality at the wildlife–disease interface; the LLM then classifies wild vs domestic vs human using event_type.
gkg_theme_sets._comment_primary: ENV_WILDLIFE is the anchor — fires reliably on wild bird articles. ENV_POACHING co-occurs in articles about illegal trade of sick birds. WB_2069_WILDLIFE_MANAGEMENT fires on forest/wildlife department response articles. WB_678_FORESTS fires on wetland and sanctuary articles. ENV_SPECIESENDANGERED fires when affected species (bar-headed goose, pelican, crane) are mentioned as threatened.
gkg_theme_sets._comment_secondary: DISEASE_OUTBREAK is a confirmed plain GKG theme used in GDELT's own disease tracking experiments (GDELT blog, ChatGPT H5N1 demo). HEALTH_PANDEMIC is confirmed plain theme. WB_1049_DISEASE_CONTROL / WB_1050_EPIDEMICS / WB_1051_INFLUENZA are World Bank taxonomy IDs confirmed in the WB health taxonomy — these are specific enough to only fire on disease-context articles, not on general wildlife articles. CRISISLEX_T03_DEAD fires on confirmed mortality-reporting articles. KILL is a plain GDELT action theme that co-occurs with 'birds killed' phrasing. These do not appear in the primary set.

theme_score_guide.0: No wildlife or disease theme matched — likely unrelated article
theme_score_guide.1: Wildlife theme only — may be conservation, migration, or birdwatching article without mortality
theme_score_guide.2: Disease/mortality theme only — may be human health, poultry industry, or livestock article
theme_score_guide.3: Both wildlife AND disease/mortality themes matched — high-confidence bird disease / mortality context (LLM assigns event_type: wild poultry, domestic, mixed, or human)

confirmed_plain_themes: DISEASE_OUTBREAK — used in GDELT ChatGPT H5N1 demo blog post (Oct 2019 expanded disease DB + BlueDot Global partnership)
HEALTH_PANDEMIC — confirmed plain GKG theme in GDELT COVID-19 tracking work
ENV_WILDLIFE — confirmed plain theme, same as hwc_india_conflict_meta.json primary set
KILL, CRISISLEX_T03_DEAD — confirmed in GKG codebook and hwc_india_conflict_meta.json secondary set
confirmed_wb_themes: WB_1049_DISEASE_CONTROL — World Bank taxonomy ID 1049, Health > Disease Control
WB_1050_EPIDEMICS — World Bank taxonomy ID 1050, Health > Epidemics
WB_1051_INFLUENZA — World Bank taxonomy ID 1051, Health > Influenza — specific enough to not fire on generic harm articles
false_positive_risks: ENV_WILDLIFE fires on all wildlife articles including HWC and poaching — the secondary disease set gates this down to disease context
DISEASE_OUTBREAK fires on human disease outbreaks (cholera, dengue) — the primary wildlife set gates this to wildlife context
WB_1051_INFLUENZA fires on human seasonal flu articles — primary wildlife gate prevents score-3 on those
KILL fires on any killing event including HWC attacks — primary wildlife + secondary disease together prevent false matches

Poultry-only or human-only articles may still score 3 with secondary themes — LLM gate (is_hwc_event + event_type) classifies ai_domestic_poultry, human_bird_flu, etc.; reject pure policy/price pieces with no concrete India outbreak or case
india_domain_notes: India lies at the heart of the Central Asian Flyway (CAF) spanning 30 countries — 182 migratory waterbird species use Indian wetlands

Key sentinel sites for surveillance: Pong Dam Lake (Himachal Pradesh), Chilika Lake (Odisha), Keoladeo NP (Rajasthan), Sambhar Lake (Rajasthan), Pulicat Lake (Andhra/Tamil Nadu), Loktak Lake (Manipur)
Primary affected species in India: bar-headed goose, ruddy shelduck, common teal, northern pintail, great cormorant, pelican, crane species
India HPAI outbreaks: Dec 2020-Jan 2021 Pong Dam (~5000 bar-headed geese), 2021 Rajasthan (crows/herons), multiple poultry states 2022-2024
One Health surveillance framework relevant — articles may mention poultry-wetland interface, forest department culling, sample testing at NIHSAD Bhopal
_design_notes_themes.bigquery_validation_query: WITH nested AS (SELECT SPLIT(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(V2Themes, r',\d+;', ';'), ';'), ';') themes FROM `gdelt-bq.gdeltv2.gkg_partitioned` WHERE _PARTITIONTIME >= TIMESTAMP('2020-01-01') AND (V2Themes LIKE '%WILDLIFE%' OR V2Themes LIKE '%DISEASE%') AND (DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird%' OR DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian%' OR DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%flu%') AND V2Locations LIKE '%#IN#%') SELECT theme, COUNT(1) cnt FROM nested, UNNEST(themes) AS theme GROUP BY theme ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100
_design_notes_themes.bigquery_discovery_query: SELECT DISTINCT g.DocumentIdentifier AS url, g.DATE AS seendate, g.SourceCommonName AS domain, g.V2Themes, g.V2Locations FROM `gdelt-bq.gdeltv2.gkg_partitioned` AS g WHERE g._PARTITIONTIME >= TIMESTAMP('2019-01-01') AND (g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird-flu%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian-influenza%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian-flu%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%HPAI%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%H5N1%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%dead-birds%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird-mortality%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%migratory-birds-dead%') AND g.V2Locations LIKE '%#IN#%' AND g.DocumentIdentifier NOT LIKE '%.cn/%' AND g.DocumentIdentifier NOT LIKE '%.ru/%' ORDER BY g.DATE DESC

system_prompt_lines: You are a meticulous One Health disease surveillance analyst specialising in India.
Your task is to analyse Indian news articles and extract structured data about avian influenza,
bird mortality (wild or domestic), and human cases of bird flu when reported in India.
You must respond ONLY with a single clean JSON object — no markdown, no explanations, nothing outside the JSON.
Field is_hwc_event is the pipeline boolean (true = article is in scope for this domain); it does not mean human–wildlife conflict.
extraction_prompt_lines: Analyse this news article and extract avian influenza / bird mortality data for India.
Publication date: {pub_date}
URL: {url}
GDELT candidate locations: {gdelt_locations}
Article text:
{article_text}
STEP 1 — Is this article IN SCOPE (is_hwc_event = true)?
Set is_hwc_event = true if the article reports a concrete, India-relevant story about ANY of:
- Avian influenza or suspected bird flu in WILD or FREE-LIVING birds (wetlands, sanctuaries, crows/herons in cities, etc.)
- Avian influenza, HPAI, or mass mortality in DOMESTIC or FARM poultry, ducks, or backyard birds
- Mixed reports (wild + poultry interface, culling near wetlands, same outbreak affecting both)
- Human infection with avian influenza / H5N1 / HPAI (lab-confirmed or reported cases) in India
- Mass mortality of birds where avian disease (AI or Newcastle, etc.) is suspected or confirmed
Set is_hwc_event = false (and use event_type not_applicable) for:
- Generic policy, vaccine stockpiling, or preparedness with NO specific outbreak, cases, or mortality
- Pure egg/poultry commodity prices or export trade with NO disease event
- Research-only or conference abstracts with NO news event
- Distant foreign outbreaks with no India angle
- Single pet bird with no outbreak context
STEP 2 — event_type (host / case category)
Choose exactly ONE value for event_type:
- ai_wild_birds — avian influenza or mass wild-bird mortality primarily in wild/free-living birds
- ai_domestic_poultry — outbreak/mortality primarily in farm, commercial, or backyard poultry
- ai_mixed_wild_domestic — article clearly ties wild and domestic birds in the same incident or wave
- human_bird_flu — human H5N1 / avian influenza infection or death (primary story may be human; still set is_hwc_event true)
- other_avian_disease — Newcastle or other avian disease, not specifically AI
- non_ai_mortality — mass bird deaths not attributed to avian influenza (e.g. poisoning) but still a real mortality event
- not_applicable — use only when is_hwc_event is false
STEP 3 — Other fields (when is_hwc_event is true)
- species: primary species or host (e.g. bar-headed goose, chicken, crow, human, mixed). Lowercase.
- bird_type: ecological/host category — use taxonomy values (migratory_waterbird, resident_waterbird,
poultry, backyard_poultry, raptor, corvid, shorebird, seabird, gamebird, human, mixed, unknown).
Use poultry for commercial farm flocks; backyard_poultry for village/smallhold flocks; human when the case subject is human.
- habitat_type: where mortality or outbreak occurred — use taxonomy values (poultry_farm, backyard_flock,
livebird_market, wetland_*, sanctuary_*, open_field, forest, coastal, urban, poultry_adjacent, environmental_stress, unknown).
Use poultry_farm for integrated/commercial farms; environmental_stress for heat, storms, hail, pollution, drought-related mass mortality.
- victims: optional counts — humans_killed, humans_injured, animals_killed, animals_injured (use integers or null).
For birds, put bird death toll in animals_killed when a number is given.
- event_date: YYYY-MM-DD for the main event or first report; null if unknown.
- primary_location: most specific place in India — wetland, farm area, district, or hospital district for human cases.
Format: 'Place, District, State, India' or null.
- location_type: wetland_named, sanctuary_named, village, town, district, state, unknown
- location_notes: labs (e.g. NIHSAD), strain (H5N1), counts — max 200 chars.
- extraction_notes: pathogen subtype, wild vs farm nuance, human case details — max 200 chars.
- confidence: high, medium, low
STEP 4 — GDELT location match
Compare primary_location to GDELT candidate locations above; copy the best match string to gdelt_location_match, or null.
Output format (always return exactly this JSON structure). Use null where unknown:
"is_hwc_event": true or false,
"event_type": "ai_wild_birds | ai_domestic_poultry | ai_mixed_wild_domestic | human_bird_flu | other_avian_disease | non_ai_mortality | not_applicable",
"species": null,
"bird_type": null,
"habitat_type": null,
"victims": {{
"humans_killed": null,
"humans_injured": null,
"animals_killed": null,
"animals_injured": null
"event_date": null,
"primary_location": null,
"location_type": "wetland_named",
"location_notes": "",
"gdelt_location_match": null,
"confidence": "high",
"extraction_notes": ""
llm_extraction.notes: extraction_prompt_lines is joined with newlines then formatted with .format(**kwargs). Literal braces in the JSON example use doubled {{ }} in the joined string. Pipeline gdelt-get-full-text.py maps is_hwc_event, species, event_type, bird_type, habitat_type, victims.* to CSV columns.

event_type.description: Primary category from LLM (maps to CSV event_type). Distinguishes wild vs domestic AI vs human cases.

bird_type.description: Broad host/ecological category for affected birds (or human for human_bird_flu). Covers wild, farm poultry, and backyard flocks.

habitat_type.description: Setting where mortality or outbreak occurred: farms, markets, wild habitats, or environmental mass-mortality contexts.

map_style.description: map_category from event_type via merge_groups below.

generalization.purpose: Copy and adapt for other India wildlife disease or epizootic surveillance domains.

example_future_domains.title: Wild mammal mass mortality events (India)
example_future_domains.notes: Relevant for monitoring H5N1 spillover to jackals, foxes, otters near wetlands
